name: anime-quiz

metadata.name: anime-quiz
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3
metadata.summary: Anime Themes Quiz for people with taste.

documentation:
## Anime Themes Quiz

**Official instance: [quiz.moe](https://quiz.moe)**

Have you ever had an anime quiz with your friends,
used a YouTube video for that and thought:

> Man, this guy has a shit taste!

Look no further!

Anime Quiz pulls the anime you and your friends watched from
your [MyAnimeList](https://myanimelist.net) profiles and randomly
generates a quiz --- it first chooses one of the participants,
then pulls their MAL list, gets links to themes hosted at
[animethemes.moe](https://animethemes.moe), plays it and gives you
10 seconds (configurable) to guess! Then the whole video is 
uncovered for you to enjoy and the person who guessed can
shout at the gamemaster to add their points to the built-in
scoreboard.

### Features

* MAL lists pulled and cached for 7 days at the server
* animethemes.moe queried and cached for 30 days
* choice of MAL lists
* choice of theme types (OP/ED)
* choice of time to guess
* all hosted online

### Run

If you just want to play, go to [quiz.moe](https://quiz.moe).

If you want to develop or self-host, the recommended way is to
install [Docker](https://docker.com) with Docker Compose and run:
```shell
docker network create server
docker-compose up -d
docker-compose exec anime_quiz python manage.py collectstatic
```

That's it. Anime Quiz will be run at http://localhost:8009.
To rebuild, run `docker-compose build` beforehand.

### Develop

PRs are welcome! The code is licensed under GNU Affero Public Licence 3.

modules.name: anime_quiz.urls
documentation:
anime_quiz URL Configuration

The `urlpatterns` list routes URLs to views. For more information please see:
    https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.1/topics/http/urls/
Examples:
Function views
    1. Add an import:  from my_app import views
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', views.home, name='home')
Class-based views
    1. Add an import:  from other_app.views import Home
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', Home.as_view(), name='home')
Including another URLconf
    1. Import the include() function: from django.urls.py import include, path
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('blog/', include('blog.urls.py'))

Dictionary returned from /anime/{slug} method.
The difference to AnimeSearchResult is that it can include additional information.

functions.name: quiz.mal.parse_priority
documentation: Parses MAL priority string into a 1-3 range

functions.name: quiz.mal.get_mal_part
documentation:
Gets a MAL list with a username.
Kwargs reffer to the arguments `(sort=3,x='w') -> ?sort=3&x=w`.
If a list is longer than 300 entries, you must set an offset.

functions.name: quiz.mal.get_raw_mal
documentation:
Gets a MAL list with a username.
Sends multiple requests if the list is longer than 300 entries.

functions.name: quiz.mal.filter_mal
documentation:
Filters a MAL list and returns a list of malids and titles.
Removes all unwanted statuses, scores, priorities.
Also filters out unreleased anime.

classes.name: quiz.tests.MyAnimeListTestCase

methods.name: quiz.tests.MyAnimeListTestCase.test_get_watching_themes
documentation:
Calls _get_user_themes and checks whether a task to pull
anime being watched by our test user is created.

methods.name: quiz.tests.MyAnimeListTestCase.test_get_completed_themes
documentation:
Calls _get_user_themes and checks whether a task to pull
anime completed by our test user is created.

methods.name: quiz.tests.MyAnimeListTestCase.test_get_watching_and_completed_themes
documentation:
Calls _get_user_themes and checks whether a task to pull
anime being watched and completed by our test user is created.

Call the function which pulls the anime to query from MAL and check
if it queued the job to get themes from animethemes.moe.

classes.name: quiz.tests.AnimeThemesMoeTestCase
documentation: Test if pulling the themes from animethemes.moe works.
